name: Pasta with Roasted Tomato and Red Chile Arrabiata
recipeIngredient: 1 large Long, red, dried New Mexican chile (about 1 1/2 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 500°F.
2. Pierce the tomatoes near their stem ends with a fork.
3. Place them in a baking pan lined with aluminum foil.
4. Put the chile in a separate foil-lined pan.
5. Place both pans in the oven.
6. Roast the chile until it turns evenly dark brown, about 5 to 7 minutes.
7. Remove the chile from the oven and set aside.
8. Continue roasting the tomatoes until their skins are evenly blackened, about 45 minutes.
9. With your hands, split open the chile and remove its stem and seeds.
10. Break the flesh and skin into a food processor fitted with the metal blade.
11. Pulse the machine on and off several times until the chile is finely chopped.
12. Add the tomatoes to the processor, including their skins and any juices that have collected in the pan.
13. Pulse the machine until the tomatoes are chopped to a coarse puree.
14. In a large saucepan or skillet, heat the oil with the garlic over moderate heat.
15. When the garlic begins to brown, add the tomato-chile mixture and stir in the sugar and dried oregano.
16. Simmer until thick but still slightly liquid, about 5 minutes.
17. Stir in the cilantro and serve over cooked pasta.
